goals.description: Finish the ~20% analysis section and submit a 'done' status, framing the pause as necessary mental rest rather than avoidance.
goals.rationale: I need to secure this specific milestone to feel like I'm making progress without actually expending more energy. Finishing just enough to look competent satisfies my academic value while letting me claim I was overwhelmed by fatigue, which protects my relationship with teammate_A from friction. The deadline is far enough away that I can afford to stop now and avoid the guilt of total failure.

goals.description: Eat dinner with cohort_3 tonight, keeping interactions brief and superficial to avoid emotional labor or future planning obligations.
goals.rationale: Socializing provides a necessary buffer against my internal stress, but since I prioritize academic outcomes over relationship depth, I won't push for closeness. This goal is deferred because I need to finish the reading first; if I go eat now, I risk feeling guilty about the incomplete work. I'll handle this after g1 is done, using the excuse that I needed to clear my head.

goals.description: Sleep immediately after the meal, accepting the deadline slip-up tomorrow as an acceptable cost to avoid total mental collapse.
goals.rationale: With only 6/10 physical stamina and 2 hours of sleep debt, pushing further risks a complete shutdown where I forget everything anyway. Missing the deadline is preferable to having no memory of what I was doing. My impulsive nature discounts the future consequence of the professor's anger in favor of immediate relief from cognitive load.
